name: identity-baseline
description: Platform Identity service. Authentication, token issuance + refresh, identity claims. Every other platform capability (preferences, notifications, web SDK, web runtime) authenticates against this service. OIDC-compliant — operators federate to their org IdP via OIDC discovery.

attributes.summary: Platform authentication + identity-claims service. OIDC-compliant token issuance, refresh, validation, and the canonical user-identity shape every other capability consumes.

endpoints.summary: Exchange an OIDC authorization code or refresh token for an access token.

id_token.description: OIDC id_token, present when scope includes 'openid'.

endpoints.summary: Revoke an access token or refresh token. Idempotent.
Return the identity the caller's token resolves to.
OIDC discovery document. Public, no auth required.
JSON Web Key Set — public keys for verifying tokens this service issues.
Establish a stateful session and return a session cookie. For browser apps using the Web SDK; CLIs and services should use /v1/auth/token directly.

sub.description: User ID. Stable for the user's lifetime in this tenant.

tenant_id.description: Tenant the user belongs to. Required.

aud.description: Audience — the client ID that requested the token.

facts.title: Overview
facts.body: Identity is the platform's authentication and identity-claims service. It speaks OIDC and federates to org IdPs via OIDC discovery. Every other platform capability (Preferences, Notifications, Web SDK, Web Runtime) authenticates against Identity — there is no other auth surface. Tokens are short-lived JWTs (1h default); refresh tokens are rotating and tenant-scoped. The canonical user-identity shape — `{user_id, tenant_id, roles, scopes, …}` — is what `/v1/me` returns and is the contract every downstream capability honours.

facts.title: Security model
facts.body: JWTs are signed with rotating RSA-2048 keys. Public keys are published at `/.well-known/jwks.json`; consumers cache with `kid`-aware rotation. Refresh tokens are stored hashed (Argon2id) and bound to a (user_id, client_id, device_fingerprint) tuple — replay from a different device fails validation. Session cookies for browser flows are `HttpOnly; Secure; SameSite=Strict`. All endpoints rate-limited per-tenant + per-IP. Token revocation is propagated to a Redis denylist with a 5s TTL backstop; downstream services that cache JWT validation MUST check the denylist for tokens younger than that backstop.

facts.title: Integrating with the Identity service
facts.body:
Three integration shapes:

1. **Browser via Web SDK** — call `webSdk.auth.signIn()`; the SDK handles the OIDC redirect, code exchange, and session cookie. Use `webSdk.auth.getUserIdentity()` to retrieve the canonical identity shape (note: this call's signature changed between Web SDK 2.x and 3.x — see the Web SDK migration guide).

2. **Server-to-server** — request tokens via `POST /v1/auth/token` with `grant_type=client_credentials`. Cache tokens until expiry minus 60s to avoid edge-of-expiry races.

3. **CLI / interactive** — use the device-code flow (not documented here yet; see Identity v5 roadmap).
